mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC < 0.02 during periods of falling FEDFUNDS over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals immediate labor market deterioration and falling consumer spending, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.5pp monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contracting industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks and rising input costs, compressing profit margins and generating negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods show no significant negative correlation with 20-day forward returns (Rank IC > -0.01) over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls directly increase aggregate household income and consumer purchasing power, creating a persistent upward trend in corporate earnings and positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth consistently coincides with negative 60-day forward returns, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, raising discount rates and prompting immediate portfolio de-risking that produces negative weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL monthly spikes above 0.4% are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 70% of observed cases, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures signals weakening domestic demand momentum, leading to earnings downgrades and negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence metric fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a t-statistic magnitude below 1.5 in out-of-sample walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects successful capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, supporting sustained corporate output growth and positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO expansion during recovery regimes yields negative 60-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the predictive coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in regime-filtered samples.
